name: sglang
_comment_c_serving: 40 µs/seq with serving_overlap_factor=0.0 and kernel_overlap_factor=0.3 — Python-heavy stack (similar to vllm). Midpoint of decode.md §7.2's 30-60 µs Python-heavy range. The Python interpreter wrapping each per-seq sampling decision breaks the CPU-runs-ahead invariant: CUDA-Graph replay does not help because Python work runs *between* graph launches, not during them. Host overhead serializes after the GPU compute window, so t_serving = c_serving·B fully blocks (ρ_serving=0). The same interpreter-stall mechanism also limits dispatch overlap to ~0.3 (lower end of the FrameworkSpec docstring's 0.3-0.6 eager-mode range for ρ_kernel).
